transcript.clean_text: stage five of the tour de france and there would be no let up of the drama this time it would all happen against the clock beginning in shanghai and finishing just down the road in laval just outside of the espace mayan in the mayan region the tour visiting for a time trial the first finish in laval for 22 years who would succeed tom stales then won in a sprint this time 27 kilometers against the clock mikhail bier was the first fast man out on the day he'd beat the time set by sweeney 52 seconds the margin and the young dame from uae looked good stefan bisaga had been one of the favorites but it started to rain and despite the brilliant save he was unable to drive it home brenda mcdoulty did crash as bisigger would be disappointingly down on the sheets 21 seconds worse off than biel matia cathagno was looking good very good in fact the former under-23 girod italia winner was making biel sweat and in fact he'd dislodge him from the hot seat that is until stefan kung came along running for group fdg in the home town of the team's owners the madurs would have been delighted when they saw the european champion come across the line beating cathanno's time by 36 second yomba vismer were in good combative mood jonas vinogor looked good not quite good enough however and primoz roglich showing not too many ill effects after his crash then came today pogaccha the tour de france winner from last year starting the day in sixth position at 39 seconds behind matchup on the pool nobody had forgotten what he did at the planche de belfie but this time trial without a climb in it and just a week or so after having come only third in the national championships eyebrows were raised when at the first and second time checks for gacha was flying the same would happen up to the finish kung unable to believe it as he looked on for gacha taking 18.2 seconds off his time and moving into the stage lead it quickly became clear that pogaccha would win the stage kung disappointed but would pugachar do enough to take the yellow jersey fanart would turn up and not provide the performance we might have been expecting ala felipe would lose over a minute and shake his head but at the start gate matier von der paul doing something he'd never done before the mountain biker the cross star the supreme king of road cycling at the moment had never ridden a time trial of this magnitude or this distance he started it by defending very well only 22 seconds down at the second intermediate 39 seconds had separated porgacha and vanderpool at the start of the day going into the final two k's there were seven seconds of a difference pogachar looked on and was enjoying the performance himself pogaccha won the stage and would not have to wear the yellow jersey this early in the tour matchup on a pool with another of those great memorable superhuman performances pogacchah winning king in second vinegar third fond de paul fifth in the time trial and still in the yellow jersey the general classification certainly looking more interesting now a lot of big gc riders defending their positions well despite crashes macho fonder pool leads going into stage six bulgachar right up their leader in the clubhouse you'd say he's at eight seconds followed by fanart and allah philippe but look out for primos roglicham richard carapaz both in the top 10 alongside rigoberto uran and on stage six a new tour de france begins a new order's been established at the top we begin in tour and head to shattu 160.6 kilometers and returning to the site of the first ever win for mark cavendish at this race in 2008 the other day he picked up stage win number 31 can stage six provide number 32 tune in to find out you
